title: Fix Cross-Tenant Admin Name Update via IDOR
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — Medium Severity**

An admin of Tenant A could update the name of Tenant B's admin account due to missing access control on the team name update endpoint. By intercepting the request and changing the `team_id` parameter to another tenant's ID, an attacker could modify another tenant's admin name.

## Affected Endpoint

`/teams/update-team-name/`

## Risk

Medium — limited to name manipulation but could disrupt business operations and naming conventions across tenants.

## Fix

Access control enforced on the endpoint to verify the requesting user belongs to the target team before allowing updates.
acceptance_criteria: Team name update endpoint validates that the requesting user belongs to the target team
Requests with mismatched team_id are rejected with 403 Forbidden
Admin can still update their own team name successfully
middleware_changes: Access control check added to team name update controller to verify the authenticated user's team membership matches the target team_id.
api_changes: POST /teams/update-team-name/ now enforces tenant-scoped authorization; returns 403 for cross-tenant requests.
implementation_notes: This IDOR vulnerability was part of a broader pattern where tenant isolation was missing on several endpoints. The fix was applied site-wide but this endpoint was initially missed.
comment_summary: Developer acknowledged the oversight and applied the tenant access control fix to staging.
